messages.content: You are given an event history generated by a Moran birth-death process. Your task is to classify whether the fixation probability rho of the mutant type is greater than or less than 0.5. rho depends on the relative fitness r and initial mutant count i0 and population size N. A value of rho > 0.5 means mutants are more likely to fix than not (label X). A value of rho < 0.5 means mutants are less likely to fix than not (label O). To estimate rho, reason through the following steps explicitly before answering: Step 1: Estimate the population size N from the trace (it is constant, visible in the N column). Step 2: Estimate the initial mutant count i0 from the earliest mutants_before value in the trace. Step 3: Estimate the relative fitness r by computing the ratio of observed mutant birth rate to the expected mutant birth rate under neutrality (mutant fraction at each step). Step 4: Use the Moran fixation formula: rho = (1 - (1/r)^i0) / (1 - (1/r)^N). Step 5: If rho > 0.5 output X, if rho < 0.5 output O. Return valid JSON with exactly these keys: label (X or O), estimated_r, estimated_i0, rho_estimated, reasoning_summary. Do not output any other text outside the JSON.

Trace file: exp001_run01.csv
Below is the observable Moran-process event history in CSV format. Classify whether the fixation probability rho is greater than 0.5 (X) or less than 0.5 (O).

step,event,birth_index,birth_type,death_index,death_type,mutants_before,mutants_after,N

8,4B:17B,4,B,17,B,1,1,20
9,8B:14B,8,B,14,B,1,1,20
10,12B:0B,12,B,0,B,1,1,20
11,15B:5B,15,B,5,B,1,1,20
12,14B:10B,14,B,10,B,1,1,20

You are given an event history generated by a Moran birth-death process. Your task is to classify whether the fixation probability rho of the mutant type is greater than or less than 0.5. rho depends on the relative fitness r and initial mutant count i0 and population size N. A value of rho > 0.5 means mutants are more likely to fix than not (label X). A value of rho < 0.5 means mutants are less likely to fix than not (label O). To estimate rho, reason through the following steps explicitly before answering: Step 1: Estimate the population size N from the trace (it is constant, visible in the N column). Step 2: Estimate the initial mutant count i0 from the earliest mutants_before value in the trace. Step 3: Estimate the relative fitness r by computing the ratio of observed mutant birth rate to the expected mutant birth rate under neutrality (mutant fraction at each step). Step 4: Use the Moran fixation formula: rho = (1 - (1/r)^i0) / (1 - (1/r)^N). Step 5: If rho > 0.5 output X, if rho < 0.5 output O. Return valid JSON with exactly these keys: label (X or O), estimated_r, estimated_i0, rho_estimated, reasoning_summary. Do not output any other text outside the JSON.
